issues.title: Phase 6.0 — audit_log generalization preflight
Global mod-list catalog dashboard (post-Phase 6, standalone repo)

comments.content:
Scoped during Phase 6 fixture-curation work as a follow-up — NOT blocking Phase 6.

Problem: there is no public AT Protocol endpoint that lets you search Bluesky moderation lists by name across all maintainers. `app.bsky.graph.getLists` is per-actor; `searchPosts` requires auth and only finds posts anyway; Ozone is operator-scoped (not a discovery layer); existing community tools (pdsls, atproto.tools, possibly blueskydirectory.com/lists/all) browse individual records but search semantics across them is unclear.

Phase 6 workaround: `tools/find-fixtures/find_fixtures.py discover` subcommand walks `getLists` for one or more user-supplied maintainers and emits a TSV catalog. Sufficient for curating ~40-60 labeled fixtures.

Proposed long-term project (separate repo, not buried in Charcoal):

1. Backfill via `com.atproto.sync.listReposByCollection?collection=app.bsky.graph.list` to enumerate every DID that has published a list, then `com.atproto.sync.getRepo` per DID to pull list records in one shot.
2. Live updates via Jetstream subscription filtered to `app.bsky.graph.list` creates/updates/deletes.
3. Index in SQLite + FTS5 over name + description + purpose (modlist / curatelist / referencelist).
4. Web dashboard with full-text search, filter by purpose / member count / labels.
5. Optionally also catalog labelers via `app.bsky.labeler.service`.

Why standalone repo rather than buried in Charcoal: other harassment-detection projects (Skywatch, similar community tools) would benefit. Decoupling it makes adoption easier and lets Charcoal consume it as a service or import the index as a static artifact.

Realistic effort: 2-4 days for a v0 (backfill + index + minimal web UI). Live updates via Jetstream add another 1-2 days.

First step before any code: confirm whether blueskydirectory.com/lists/all already has working search — if so, this whole project is unnecessary. Bryan to spot-check in browser.

issues.title: Investigate ONNX inference failures during long-running scans

comments.content:
Observed during the 2026-06-05 grimalkina.bsky.social scan (#182):

- 12 ONNX inference failures over a 2h 34min scan
- Source: src/pipeline/amplification.rs WARN "Failed to score follower, skipping" with error="ONNX inference failed"
- Pattern: sparse, scattered throughout the scan (not clustered)
- Effect: affected followers skipped entirely — counted in completion `accounts=` total but no score row written
- Benign on a small scale (12 / ~3300 = 0.4%), but worth investigating if rate scales with scan length

Possible causes:
1. Memory pressure under long-running scan — ort session may degrade after many calls
2. Specific post content triggers tokenizer/model edge case (UTF-8, length, repeated chars)
3. ort 2.0.0-rc.11 session pool exhaustion / locking issue
4. Tokenizers crate bug on certain inputs

Investigation plan:
1. Add post-content snapshot logging when ONNX fails — capture text length, presence of non-BMP unicode, language markers
2. Track memory usage on Railway during scan (current Phase 5 scans are RAM-heavy due to Zentropi + NLI + ONNX in parallel)
3. Confirm 12 failures are random vs. correlated (same follower? same post pattern?)
4. Consider rebuilding ort session periodically (every N inferences) as defensive measure

Low priority unless ONNX failure rate grows with scan size.

issues.title: Investigate sporadic Zentropi 403 Forbidden errors during long scans

comments.content:
Observed during the 2026-06-05 grimalkina.bsky.social scan (#182):

- 45 Zentropi failures over a 2h 34min scan, all HTTP 403 Forbidden
- Response body is HTML (`<!doctype html>...<title>403</title>403 Forbidden`), NOT a JSON error body — suggests block at CDN/proxy layer (Cloudflare/CloudFront/WAF) rather than Zentropi service itself
- First 403 hit at 06:09:54Z, 2h 19min into the scan — late-onset, not present at scan start
- Sporadic after that (45 over remaining ~15 min)
- Code path: src/toxicity/ensemble.rs WARN "Zentropi failed, falling back to ONNX threshold" — fallback works, no scoring impact, but bypasses Zentropi for affected posts

Contrast with chaosgreml.in 2026-05-17 scan: hundreds-to-thousands of 429s, no 403s.

Hypotheses to investigate:
1. Zentropi auth token (API key) has a TTL or rotation — may invalidate mid-scan
2. Upstream CDN (Cloudflare?) blocks long-lived connections or specific IP patterns after sustained usage
3. Zentropi changed billing/auth — sporadic 403 may indicate quota/credit exhaustion partway through
4. Specific labeler version (ZENTROPI_LABELER_VERSION_ID) deprecated mid-scan

Next steps:
1. Run `charcoal zentropi-check` to confirm current auth health
2. Add 403-specific logging: capture response headers (especially `cf-ray`, `x-amz-cf-id`, `server`, `www-authenticate`) to identify whether the block is at CDN or origin
3. Implement bounded retry with backoff on 403 (currently we just fall back to ONNX)
4. Consider env-var rotation strategy if auth has a TTL

Related: #181 (Zentropi concurrency) — original 429-rate-limit concern not reproducing at 5x the load; this 403 pattern is the new failure mode worth tracking.

issues.title: Investigate Zentropi API call concurrency pattern

comments.content:
Findings:

Zentropi calls are parallel on both axes.

Client: src/toxicity/zentropi.rs
- ZentropiClient::classify(text) — single post
- ZentropiClient::classify_pair(parent, reply) — reply-in-context

Batch fan-out: src/toxicity/ensemble.rs:177-215
- classify_batch_with_contexts() uses stream::iter(...).buffer_unordered(ZENTROPI_CONCURRENCY)
- ZENTROPI_CONCURRENCY = 4 — up to 4 in-flight Zentropi calls per account

Account fan-out: src/pipeline/sweep.rs:134-159
- Sweep stream uses buffer_unordered(concurrency), default 8 from CLI

Peak: ~8 accounts × 4 posts = ~32 concurrent Zentropi requests.

No rate-limit handling visible in zentropi.rs — worth a follow-up if Zentropi has per-key concurrency caps. Closing as informational.

issues.title: Address deferred CodeRabbit findings from PR #47 (non-blocking)

comments.content:
CodeRabbit reviewed PR #47 (staging->main promotion) on 2026-05-17. The critical findings were fixed in a follow-up PR. These are real but non-blocking; address as a tech-debt sweep when capacity allows.

**Robustness / correctness:**
- `src/scoring/nli.rs` — `softmax_entailment` panics if logits.len() < 2; return Result or Option instead
- `src/scoring/threat.rs` — clamp `context_score` to [0.0, 1.0] before computing `context_multiplier` to prevent under/over-amplification
- `src/scoring/nli_audit.rs` — `append_jsonl` has an unsynchronized read/rotate/open/write race under concurrent NLI scoring; wrap with file lock
- `src/scoring/nli_audit.rs` — add `user_did` field to `NliAuditEntry` for multi-user audit attribution
- `src/main.rs` ~line 1164 — `?` on `config.zentropi_api_key` short-circuits the partial-config warning; read into Options and check together
- `src/main.rs` ~line 318 — `fetch_recent_posts` errors silently swallowed by `unwrap_or_default`; log before fallback
- `src/pipeline/amplification.rs` ~line 89 — `amplifier_text` only fetched when `analyze_followers` is true; in Phase B that path is missed
- `src/pipeline/amplification.rs` ~line 278 — duplicate amplifier DIDs cause duplicate follower fetches; dedupe via HashSet
- `src/bluesky/posts.rs` ~line 267 — `reply_ratio`/`quote_ratio` computed against `total_collected` (post-filter); introduce `total_authored` (pre-filter) for unbiased ratios
- `src/bluesky/relationships.rs` ~line 131 — parse failure skips inserting entries for target_dids, leaving missing entries downstream; insert a sane default
- `src/bluesky/replies.rs` ~line 49 — thread extraction pushes entries with empty URI; add `uri.is_empty()` check
- `src/constellation/client.rs` ~line 170 — `find_likers` fetches only first page; loop with cursor until exhausted
- `src/discovery/topic_search.rs` ~line 56 — `search_posts_for_authors` counts already_scored DIDs against `max_results` budget; filter before counting

**Data integrity:**
- `src/db/queries.rs` ~line 723 — `insert_inferred_pair` uses `last_insert_rowid()` after `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`, returning wrong id on conflict; use RETURNING id (SQLite path, dev-only)
- `src/db/traits.rs` ~line 187 — `list_users` and `update_last_login` lack `user_did: &str` first parameter required by multi-user trait convention
- `src/db/schema.rs` ~line 217 — SQLite migrations run DDL and schema_version updates non-atomically across v5-v8 closures; wrap in SAVEPOINT or make idempotent

**Documentation:**
- `README.md` ~line 171 — describes OpenAI ensemble; replace with Zentropi binary classifier (TwoStageToxicityScorer behavior, fallback semantics, env vars)

**Migration policy:**
- `migrations/postgres/0005_contextual_scoring.sql` ~line 16 — `user_labels.label` accepts arbitrary text; add ENUM or CHECK constraint for the documented label set

**Tooling / dev scripts:**
- `.gitignore` line 31 — `.chainlink/issues-export.json` is ignored AND tracked; reconcile (untrack via `git rm --cached` OR remove from .gitignore)
- `scripts/check-updates.sh` ~line 29 — `crate_latest()` and `tool_installed_version()` grep pipelines need `|| true` to survive `set -euo pipefail` on no-match
- `scripts/check-updates.sh` ~line 205 — `pinned_rev`/`short_rev` are hardcoded; parse from Cargo.toml

**Tests:**
- `src/output/markdown.rs` ~line 245 — test fixtures use `graph_distance: None`; flip one to `Some(...)` and assert the "Distance" column renders

issues.title: Triage CodeRabbit review on PR #47 (staging→main promotion)
Audit pre-commit/pre-push hook: fmt+clippy drift slipped through to CI twice on PR #47

comments.content:
Two distinct gaps surfaced during the PR #47 (staging->main) promotion on 2026-05-17:

**1. Local toolchain drift**: local cargo is on Rust 1.94, CI is on Rust 1.95. Two new clippy lints (useless_conversion, unnecessary_sort_by) fire under 1.95 but not 1.94 — pre-commit/pre-push gates passed locally, CI failed. Rustfmt opinion also drifted between versions, causing a separate CI fmt failure even after the clippy fix landed.

**2. Pre-push "no Rust changes" false negative**: the push of fix/rustfmt-after-clippy produced this output before the gate-skip:
```
fatal: ambiguous argument origin/fix/rustfmt-after-clippy..HEAD: unknown revision or path not in the working tree.
Pre-push: no Rust changes, skipping quality gate.
```
The hook diffed against an upstream tracking branch that did not yet exist (new branch first push). It errored, swallowed the error, and defaulted to "no Rust changes" — which was wrong; this push contained a Rust file change. The skip path needs to detect the "no upstream yet" case and fall back to diffing against the merge-base of staging/main instead.

**Suggested fixes**:
- Pin local toolchain: add `rust-toolchain.toml` at repo root with `channel = "stable"` AND run `rustup update stable` regularly, OR pin to a specific version that matches CI
- Fix pre-push: when no upstream exists, diff against `git merge-base HEAD origin/staging` (or origin/main) to detect changes being pushed for the first time
- Consider running fmt/clippy in pre-push unconditionally on any Rust-touching commit reachable from HEAD but not from origin/staging

**Impact**: cost two extra CI cycles and two extra fix PRs (#48 clippy, #49 fmt) to land the staging->main promotion.

Additional observation from 2026-05-17 session: the bug appears to trigger only on **truly-new-everywhere** branches. When pushing local `staging` (which had a stale tracking ref to a now-deleted `origin/staging`), the pre-push hook **did** run the quality gate normally — clippy + 419 tests, all passed — even though the push created a brand new `staging` ref on the remote.

Hypothesis: the failing path is `git rev-list origin/<branch>..HEAD` when `origin/<branch>` does not exist as a ref. The staging push worked because the local-tracking ref `refs/remotes/origin/staging` still existed from before deletion (git keeps local tracking refs until next `git fetch --prune`), so the rev-list succeeded against the stale-but-present ref.

This narrows the repro: the bug needs a branch whose remote tracking ref has **never existed locally**, which matches every first-push of a freshly-created `fix/*` branch. Suggests the fix is to detect that case specifically and fall back to `git merge-base HEAD origin/main` (or origin/staging) as the diff base.

Encountered 4 times today (PR #48, #49, #50, #51 first-pushes) and zero times on the staging push.

comments.content:
Now that the NLI logit index is fixed (#113), context_scores are producing real signal. Examples from staging scan 2026-03-21:

- amos-race.bsky.social: hostility 0.541 (attack 0.584, contempt 0.516) — BUT in Low tier because base toxicity is low
- andyschwab.link: hostility 0.380 (misrepresent 0.616, good_faith 0.471) — also Low tier

The problem: context_multiplier range is 1.0-1.5x, so a Low-tier account with raw_score 3.0 and extreme context (1.0) only gets boosted to 4.5 — still Low. Someone who is specifically hostile to Bryan's content but generally non-toxic flies under the radar.

Options to evaluate:
1. Widen multiplier range (1.0-2.0x or 1.0-2.5x) — simple, keeps multiplicative property
2. Add context as independent signal (e.g. if context_score > 0.5, floor raw_score at Watch threshold) — catches concern trolls but risks false positives
3. Hybrid: use higher multiplier AND lower the Watch threshold when context_score is high
4. Keep as-is — the current design correctly says 'low general toxicity = low threat even with hostile interactions'

Key question for Bryan: is someone who is ONLY hostile toward your content but polite everywhere else a threat worth flagging? If yes, option 2 or 3. If no, option 4 is correct.

comments.content:
Code review findings from PR #6 (SQLite-to-PostgreSQL). Fix before merge.

CRITICAL:
1. Migration race condition: run_migrations() has no transaction wrapping. Partial failure leaves DB corrupt. Wrap each migration in BEGIN/COMMIT. Note: CREATE EXTENSION cannot run in a transaction — handle migration 1 specially.
2. Fragile interval in is_score_stale: uses string concat ($2 || ' days'). Replace with make_interval(days => $2) and bind i32 directly.

HIGH:
3. status::show breaks for Postgres: checks SQLite file existence, prints 'not initialized' when no .db file exists. Need backend-aware status display.
4. u32::MAX to i32 overflow: migrate command calls get_recent_events(u32::MAX), cast to i32 wraps to -1. Cap at i32::MAX or add get_all_events method.
5. Migration only copies 2 hardcoded scan_state keys: add get_all_scan_state() to trait or query SQLite directly.
6. Migration loses event timestamps/IDs: insert_amplification_event sets detected_at=NOW(). All migrated events get same timestamp, breaking pile-on detection. Need raw INSERT preserving original timestamps.

MEDIUM (fix later):
7. save_embedding UPDATE silently does nothing if no fingerprint row exists
8. No concurrent migration protection (advisory locks)
9. CREATE EXTENSION vector needs superuser (document requirement)
10. Postgres tests dont clean up (consider sqlx::test or transaction rollback)
11. post_count as i32 could overflow
12. sqlite feature flag defined but never gated

issues.description:
Detect amplification attacks where users screenshot posts instead of quoting/reposting them. This bypasses Constellation and AT Protocol backlinks entirely.

**Phase 1: Text-based search (searchPosts)**
Use app.bsky.feed.searchPosts to find posts mentioning the protected user's handle as plain text (not @mentions). Attackers who screenshot posts almost always reference the handle in their commentary. Low-cost, catches the majority of cases. Known example: @aly.codes was screenshotting posts for toxic amplification.

**Phase 2: Image OCR (production-informed)**
For high-signal situations learned from production usage, add OCR scanning of attached images to detect:
- Protected user's handle visible in screenshots
- Protected user's post text matched in screenshot content
Consider local ONNX OCR model (tesseract or similar) to stay consistent with the local-first approach. Only activate for situations where Phase 1 misses attacks that are confirmed through other channels.

**Notes:**
- Phase 2 scope should be shaped by production data — what patterns do screenshot attackers actually follow?
- OCR is expensive; may want to limit to posts from accounts already flagged or from known hostile amplification chains
- Deciduous nodes: 100 (goal), 101 (decision)

Recalibrate threat scoring for sentence embedding overlap scale

comments.content:
Problem: With sentence embeddings, topic overlap values are 0.45-0.85 for most accounts in the social neighborhood, vs 0.05-0.24 with TF-IDF. The current formula (toxicity*70 + overlap*30) gives scores of 15-25 from overlap alone, putting nearly everyone at Elevated or higher regardless of toxicity.

Real data from fresh scan: 12 High, 160 Elevated, 32 Watch, 9 Low out of 213 accounts. Most 'High' accounts have toxicity <0.10 — they're allies.

Fix approach: 
1. Change formula so toxicity is the PRIMARY driver and overlap is a multiplier/modifier rather than additive
2. New formula: score = toxicity_component * (1 + overlap_boost). High overlap with low toxicity = low score. High overlap WITH toxicity = amplified score.
3. Raise tier thresholds to reflect real-world score distributions
4. Keep the overlap gate (below threshold = capped) but adjust threshold for embedding scale
5. Update all unit tests and composition tests to match new formula

issues.description:
Replace notification-based amplification detection with Constellation's public graph API. This enables scanning any public user without their credentials.

Key requirements:
- Constellation API for detecting quote/repost events on any user's posts
- protected_users table: list of users we actively monitor (manual add or signup)
- Per-user topic fingerprints (each protected user has their own)
- Shared toxicity scores across all protected users (scored once, reused)
- Threat scores combine shared toxicity with per-user topic overlap
- Users should not be added to protection until they sign up or are manually added

Scaling & accuracy:
- Tiered scoring depth: broad shallow pass (20 posts), deepen Watch-tier to 100+
- Background job for new user onboarding (build fingerprint, then sweep network async)
- Higher concurrency for network-bound work (16-32 vs current 8)
- Shared toxicity is the biggest scaling win — score once, compute overlap per-user cheaply

Fingerprint & score lifecycle:
- Full fingerprints for protected users and targets, refreshed periodically with new posts only (incremental, not full rebuild)
- Toxicity scores refresh the same way — add new post scores incrementally
- CRITICAL: never overwrite scores that a user has already acted on (blocked, muted, reviewed). Historical basis for actions must be preserved. Score history or versioning needed.
- Consider: action_log table linking user actions to the score snapshot that informed them

Some of this may be v2 (score versioning, incremental refresh) vs v1 (Constellation + multi-user + protected_users table).

Supersedes #19 (configurable protected user).
See docs/research-alternative-approaches.md for Constellation discussion.

issues.title: Select and implement Perspective API replacement

comments.content: Research complete (issue #11). Full report at docs/toxicity-alternatives-report.md. Top recommendation: Detoxify unbiased-toxic-roberta via ONNX Runtime (ort crate). Backup: OpenAI Moderation API. Future complement: CoPE by Zentropi for policy-steerable edge cases. This issue blocks scaling the MVP since Perspective API has a 1 QPS rate limit and sunsets Dec 31, 2026.

DECISION POINTS:

1. Primary scorer: Detoxify unbiased-toxic-roberta via ONNX (recommended) vs alternatives
   - Returns continuous 0-1 scores across 7 categories (matches existing ToxicityScorer trait)
   - Identity-bias mitigation (critical for Bryan's topic areas: fat liberation, queer identity, DEI)
   - Pre-exported ONNX at protectai/unbiased-toxic-roberta-onnx (~125 MB quantized)
   - Runs on CPU, no API dependency, Apache 2.0
   - Alternative: MiniLMv2-toxic-jigsaw-onnx (22M params, even smaller, ROC-AUC 0.98)

2. Integration approach: ort crate (pure Rust ONNX Runtime bindings) recommended
   - Production-proven (SurrealDB, Bloop, Google Magika)
   - Pair with tokenizers crate for text tokenization
   - Alternative: rust-bert (adds ~2GB libtorch dep), candle (pure Rust but more manual work)

3. Fallback strategy: OpenAI Moderation API as cloud backup
   - Free, 13 harm categories, 95% accuracy, easy reqwest integration
   - Useful when local inference isn't available or for cross-validation

4. Timeline: Replace now vs keep Perspective and swap later
   - Perspective works until Dec 31, 2026, but has 1 QPS rate limit blocking pipeline scaling
   - Local ONNX scorer eliminates rate limits entirely
   - Replacing now unblocks issues #9 (rate limiting) and #10 (pipeline scaling)

RELATED ISSUES:
- #9 (rate limiting) becomes moot with local scorer
- #10 (pipeline scaling) partially unblocked by removing API rate limits
- #11 (research) completed, report at docs/toxicity-alternatives-report.md

Implementation complete. Added OnnxToxicityScorer using Detoxify unbiased-toxic-roberta model via ort crate. New files: src/toxicity/onnx.rs (scorer), src/toxicity/download.rs (model download helper).
